extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the 31.9km ITT by 2 seconds over VAN AERT Wout, who lost 22 seconds in the middle sector after dominating early.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT lost 22 seconds between splits 1 and 2, then clawed back 9 in final sector—revealing pacing vulnerability mid-effort.
ROGLIC placed 5th as best GC contender, beating VINGEGAARD by 2 places—signals superior TT form entering Grand Tours.
CATTANEO took 3rd place and 2nd GC at 53 seconds—QuickStep's hidden ITT weapon unexpectedly competitive against specialists.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: dominated first split by 11 seconds then collapsed in middle sector, suggesting pacing miscalculation or premature exertion
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: 3rd place in national champ skinsuit at 1:40, strong enough to take white jersey with 5th GC
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: 5th place beats fellow GC men VINGEGAARD and CARAPAZ, sits 3rd GC at 56 seconds behind VAN AERT
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: 7th place adequate but beaten by ROGLIC, starts 56 seconds down on GC in 4th
rider_signals.CATTANEO Matteo: positive: 4th place ITT performance vaults him to 2nd GC, QuickStep domestique unexpectedly competitive against TT specialists
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: 5th fastest at second intermediate split indicates sustained power, finished top-10
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: maintained 53.9 km/h average despite technical opening, second split 39 seconds faster than DURBRIDGE benchmark
